basics.name: Brendan Carpio
basics.label: Software Craftsman: Writing software you can forget about | Linux, containers, CLI
basics.summary: Senior Software Engineer and software craftsman at Microsoft: an SRE-minded, Linux-native polyglot who led large-scale platform migrations, became the team's Kubernetes SME, and architected caching strategies that slashed calls to high-cost backend services by 90%, yielding ~$6M/year in savings. I build systems that self-heal and scale gracefully so people can rest easy.

work.name: Microsoft
work.summary: Technical lead for Azure Maps routing migration, caching architecture, and platform scaling across distributed scheduling services.
highlights: Drove >$6M/year in cost savings and reduced route API consumption by 97% by designing and implementing a distributed Redis + application-layer caching system.
Led end-to-end migration of spatial data providers, increasing performance capacity by 100x while maintaining service health across 7+ staged rollouts.
Iterated on distributed cache and spatial data provider code to reduce cache memory by 50%, pod memory by 82%, and increase throughput by 100% — eliminating OOM errors and lowering COGS.
Solved QPS bottlenecks and throughput constraints by designing distributed rate-limiting and throttling systems across multi-pod AKS deployments.
Built end-to-end observability for Redis health, cache efficiency, and Azure Maps usage using Grafana and Kusto/ADX.
Prototyped LLM skills to automatically generate Grafana charts from internal Azure resource metrics and code repo logs.
Led cross-team alignment across Maps, URS, SOA, Redis, and platform teams to unblock migration and scaling challenges

work.name: Microsoft
work.summary: Sole Kubernetes engineer and DevOps SME responsible for containerization, deployment, and platform delivery across scheduling services.
highlights: Served as team Kubernetes SME — maintained Helm charts, designed low/no-touch cluster scaling for stateful applications, and architected a multi-cluster orchestration service for dynamic K8s deployment with per-customer configuration.
Improved AKS deployment reliability by building rollback diagnostic workflows that cut time-to-root-cause for failed deployments.
Designed and automated a CVE patching pipeline that kept containers current without manual intervention, reducing security toil across all deployments.
Drove upstream platform changes that improved performance and reliability for multiple dependent teams.
Promoted Docker best practices across the team through brown bags on container internals and standardized hygiene guidelines.
Presented intermediate Kubernetes concepts — auto-healing, monitoring, and scaling — to an audience of 100+ engineers.

work.name: Microsoft
work.summary: Built deployment infrastructure and IoT container systems for Connected Store / Connected Spaces — a computer vision platform enabling retail businesses to track customer behavior and surface real-time worker alerts.
highlights: Designed the majority of K8s infrastructure for the product — Helm charts, deployment scripts, sidecars, and tooling evaluation — as one of a small group of Kubernetes-focused engineers on the team.
Built Bash-based deployment automation from scratch for Azure Stack Edge, replacing manual processes across ~30 edge devices.
Took full ownership of the Python-based IoT container, maintaining and evolving core functionality for the computer vision pipeline.
Assisted other teams with Docker container design to ensure cross-service compatibility and runtime hygiene.
Established the Kubernetes and Linux expertise that became the foundation for later platform engineering work across the team

work.name: Humana
work.position: DevOps + Microservice + Cloud Infrastrucure Software Development Extended Intern
highlights: Developed a microservice using Node.js and Azure DevOps APIs to automate the download and upload of certificates between Google Cloud Platform's App Engine and Azure DevOps pipelines, reducing deployment time and manual intervention for CI/CD setup.
Utilized the Serverless framework to package and deploy functions in a platform-agnostic manner, ensuring consistent tooling and ease of use regardless of cloud provider.
Worked with Node.js, Azure DevOps, and Google Cloud Platform App Engine.

work.name: Humana
highlights: Developed a Project Estimate Web App that replaced mass emailed Excel spreadsheets, reducing errors and completion time by 50%. Connected to a Flask API hosted on Google Cloud Platform, the app provided a centralized infrastructure for project cost calculations with version tracking. I was primarily responsible for vue.js front-end development and configuring Azure Dev Ops pipelines.
Collaborated with an Agile: Scrum team, splitting focus between front-end and back-end halves. Ensured routine communication was maintained between the two halves to meet Service Level Agreements.
Organized and ran a company Hackathon, showcasing leadership and organizational skills. Coordinated logistics such as food, judges, room, and prompts.

work.name: Union Pacific Railroad
work.position: Machine Learning + Java/Python Microservice Software Development Intern
highlights: Developed two APIs, one in Java with Spring Boot and one in Python with Django and spaCy, to streamline help ticket processing and routing.
Worked closely with a partner to receive daily feedback and ensure timely completion of projects.

Successfully implemented an API "glue" using Java to connect Python, Solr, and ColdFusion front-end, significantly improving user experience by grouping, classifying, and redirecting help tickets using machine learning algorithms.

work.name: Michigan State University
work.position: Full-stack Java Spring Software Development Intern
highlights: Collaborated with a development team to create and maintain full-stack web applications using Java with Spring Boot, jQuery, and Hibernate, ensuring timely completion of assigned "stories".

Contributed to the development of the ProSAM Administrative Web Application, allowing for no-code editing of financial aid calculation variables with input validation and surfacing errors during batch processing on a user-friendly dashboard.

projects.name: Michigan State Ballroom Dance Team Website
projects.description: As a team member of the Ballroom Dance Team, I noticed that our website was looking a bit outdated. I took it upon myself to design a new one that could be easily managed by none tech-savvy people and which would be better suited for the following years.   Starting completely from scratch, I've been building the website with HTML5, CSS3, and Bootstrap 3. After completing the base, data from the old website was migrated and reformatted into a more presentable format.

projects.name: exNoops: Elixir API wrapper for Github's Noops
projects.description: Simple API wrapper for Github’s Noops (read: toy APIs). Developers can quickly and easily import this package; use idiomatic function calls to query the API, and get back native Elixir objects with minimal processing.
highlights: 100% native Elixir, minimal dependencies, TDD w/ automated pipelines to compile, test, and deploy.

projects.name: muFind: 2019 Humanathon Winner
projects.description: *Ionic Framework, Golang, MongoDB, Node.JS, Google Cloud Platform *Full stack, cloud based, CSR application that helps users locate a desk or room on a floor plan. Humana makes it easy to locate what floor a conference room or person's desk is located. However, once you're there it's common to just walk around the floor looking for the room.  *Web interface and native mobile apps allows a Google Maps like experience. Searching for a location shows a pin on a floor plan map and gently zooms in.
